---
title: "Method-path coverage"
description: "An honest account of which identification paths RECAST has actually been run on — and which it has not."
---

RECAST's estimand router has branches for more designs than have been exercised
end-to-end. This page states plainly what is validated and what is not, so the
"validated" framing elsewhere on the site is never read as a claim of full
coverage.

## What has been exercised

| Method path | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| PLR / OLS, continuous treatment | ✅ Exercised | [Nunn & Trefler](../papers/nunn_trefler_2010/) (bit-for-bit regression test), [Djankov](../papers/djankov_taxes_2010/) (benchmarked + control discipline) |
| IRM + generic ML, binary RCT | ✅ Exercised | [Teacher training](../papers/teacher_training_2019/) |
| IV / PLIV, continuous treatment | ✅ Exercised | [Ashraf & Galor](../papers/ashraf_galor_2013/) — replication exact; extension functional-form-limited (and labeled so) |
| Blocking replication gate (replicate-or-don't-extend) | ✅ Exercised | a fault-injection `FAIL` (sign-flipped target) halts before the extension; the gate also `BLOCKED`s when the paper is missing or no published number exists to match — no fabrication, no extension |
| Control discipline / suspect controls | ✅ Exercised | [Djankov](../papers/djankov_taxes_2010/) — 6 of 12 controls flagged |
| Continuous-treatment DiD → PLR on two-way-FE panel (`DID_PLR`) | ✅ Exercised | [Trust / FinTech](../papers/trust_fintech_2025/) — open-access; published Table 5 triple-diff reproduced within ~2% (gate PASS), then DML extension 0.014–0.022 |
| Staggered binary DiD → `DoubleMLDIDMulti` | ⚠️ Exercised (with caveat) | [Divine Policy](../papers/divine_policy_2025/) — the CS estimator is infeasible at 50 states; the pipeline falls back to `DID_PLR` and says so |
| Heterogeneity: **GATE / CATE** via DoubleML `gate()`/`cate()` (PLR · IRM · DID_PLR) | ✅ Exercised | [Nunn & Trefler](../papers/nunn_trefler_2010/) (PLR, `init`/`human_cap`), [Trust / FinTech](../papers/trust_fintech_2025/) (DID_PLR, group ATE by `NonRep` — the paper's own triple-diff dimension); IRM branch synthetic-tested |
| Heterogeneity: **generic-ML GATES/BLP/CLAN** (CDDF 2018, RCT) | ✅ Exercised | [Teacher training](../papers/teacher_training_2019/) — the benchmarkable sorted-group analysis |
| Heterogeneity: **DiD group-time ATTs** (Callaway–Sant'Anna `aggregate`) | ⚠️ Wired; not feasible on current papers | [Divine](../papers/divine_policy_2025/) at 50 states → DID_PLR fallback, so group-time cells are reported as unavailable; the decomposition is wired + synthetic-tested |

All HTE runs above flow through **one uniform dispatcher** (`lib/recast_dml.run_heterogeneity`) that picks the standard DoubleML tool per design — `gate()`/`cate()` for PLR/IRM/DID_PLR, group-time ATTs for staggered DiD — and emits an explicit *not-applicable* verdict where DoubleML has no native gate/cate (PLIV/IIVM/APOS). Moderators are always **pre-declared** from the paper's own heterogeneity discussion (no moderator shopping).

## What has *not* been tested

| Method path | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Binary IV → IIVM / LATE | ❌ Not yet tested | The router branch exists; no test case has run it. |
| Multivalued treatment → APOS | ❌ Not yet tested | Branch exists; unexercised. |
| Staggered binary DiD on a *large* panel | ❌ Not yet tested | `DoubleMLDIDMulti` ran only on synthetic data + the small-N Divine fallback; an N-large staggered paper would exercise it natively. |

## Faithfulness is not generality

A regression test proves the automation is **faithful** — on Nunn & Trefler it
reproduced a hand-built analysis bit-for-bit. It cannot prove the automation is
**general**: that one paper has no fixed effects, runs a penalized learner, uses
PLR (not IRM or PLIV), and uses `gate()`/`cate()` (not generic ML), so it cannot
trigger the bugs that live on the other paths. Every additional paper on a new path
has surfaced real framework issues that were then fixed — which is exactly why the
honest reading of the table above is "these five behaviors are validated," not
"RECAST handles everything."

## Scouting new candidates

Beyond these fixed examples, RECAST continuously **scouts** newly published papers
that have an available replication package and screens whether each one's
identification design falls on a path it supports. Every paper looked at — accepted
*and* rejected — is logged on the [Candidate scouting](../candidates.qmd) page, so the
pipeline of what might be RECASTed next is auditable rather than cherry-picked.
